LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CAROLYN WANG'S MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION. THE MATTER IS CALLED. ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH A MODIFICATION STRIKING THE LANGUAGE REGARDING PROFESSIONAL COURTESY: THE JUDGE PRO TEM'S REPORT INDICATES THAT THE PARTIES AGREED THE AT-ISSUE DEPOSITIONS WOULD BE HELD MARCH 3, 2021, SO, AS THE REPORT ALSO INDICATES, THE MOTION TO COMPEL THOSE DEPOSITIONS APPEARS TO BE MOOT. I ADOPT THE JUDGE PRO TEM'S RECOMMENDATION THAT SANCTIONS BE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED AS MODIFIED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-01-2021 Vacated

MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION
Off Calendar Mar-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Nicholas Carlin, Esq. of Phillips Erlewine Given Carlin LLP, 415.398.0900, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Anthony Bettencourt, Esq. of Geary Shea O'Donnell Grattan & Mitchell, 707.545.1600, for the Defendants.

PLAINTIFF CAROLYN WANG'S MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with a modification striking the language regarding professional courtesy:

The judge pro tem's report indicates that the parties agreed the at-issue depositions would be held March 3, 2021, so, as the report also indicates, the motion to compel those depositions appears to be moot. I adopt the judge pro tem's recommendation that sanctions be denied.

Order signed as modified.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-23-2021 Continued To Sep-20-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION
Off Calendar Feb-26-2021 Continued To Mar-12-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Michael D. Levinson (mdl@phillaw.com) and Nicholas A. Carlin (NAC@phillaw.com) of Phillips, Erlewine, Given & Carlin LLP for Plaintiff.
Sterling Tipton (stipton@gearylaw.com) of Geary, Shea, O'Donnell, Grattan & Mitchell, PC for Defendants.

Counsel do(es) not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CAROLYN WANG's MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION:

The matter is continued to March 12, 2021 at 9:30 am in Department 302, Law and Motion Department.
Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: Jeff Wohl, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
